original_abstract: Character education is a vital pillar in sustaining national integrity, particularly in addressing modern moral challenges such as delinquency, social unrest, and shifting ethical standards. This study investigates the implementation of Islamic character values through the habituation method at SDN Babakan Sirna, Sukabumi. Using a qualitative descriptive approach, the research explores the objectives, forms, evaluation mechanisms, and influencing factors of this pedagogical strategy. Data were gathered through interviews, site observations, and documentation. The findings indicate that habituation is systematically applied across four domains: daily ethics (akhlaq), religious practice (ibadah), annual Islamic activities, and nationalistic engagement. These practices have fostered key traits in students, including religious devotion, discipline, social empathy, and environmental awareness. Evaluation is conducted through routine assemblies, teacher coordination meetings, and structured student conduct records. While the program benefits from strong leadership, professional faculty, and adequate facilities, challenges remain regarding inter-teacher coordination, student personality diversity, and the establishment of a consistent disciplinary culture. The study concludes that the habituation method serves as an effective instrument for internalizing Islamic values, transforming them from mere theoretical concepts into consistent behavioral patterns.
humanized_abstract: Character education is very significant to maintain the national integrity to face new challenges of morality such as the occurrence of delinquency, social unrest and moral decline. This study aims to examine the implementation of Islamic character values through habituation method at SDN Babakan Sirna, Sukabumi. Methodologically, this study uses qualitative descriptive approach to investigate the purpose, structure, mechanism of evaluation and implementation of character education through habituation method. The data was collected through interview, observation and documentation. The results show that the habituation method is implemented in four aspects, namely daily akhlaq (moral character), ibadah (worship), annual Islamic celebration and nationalistic education. Students are able to develop and enhance their religious ibadah, discipline, empathy to others and environmental consciousness. The evaluation of the character education is carried out through the morning talk, teacher meeting and students performance record. The implementation of character education through habituation method in this school is supported by the school administrator, teachers competence, physical building and infrastructure. However, the implementation is hampered by lack of unity among teachers, students' personality and discipline. Therefore, the habituation method is very effective to promote Islamic character values into concrete actions.
